title: WOOF Q4 Deep Dive: Margin Expansion and Strategic Store Initiatives Offset Revenue Decline
text: WOOF Q4 Analysis: Margin Expansion Amid Revenue Decline. Petco (NASDAQ: WOOF) delivered mixed Q4 results, meeting revenue expectations while facing a 2.4% year-over-year sales decline to $1.52 billion. However, the company impressed investors with improved profitability metrics. Financial Performance: The retailer's adjusted earnings per share matched analyst estimates at $0.04, while adjusted EBITDA of $106.3 million surpassed expectations by 12.9%. CEO Joel Anderson emphasized operational improvements, stating the company achieved a healthier economic model through disciplined cost controls. Strategic Initiatives Driving Results: Management attributed margin gains to three key actions: Store Optimization, Petco closed underperforming locations, reducing stores from 1,398 to 1,382 year-over-year. CFO Sabrina Simmons noted favorable rent negotiations allowed some locations to remain open with better economics. Inventory Discipline - The company reduced inventory by nearly 10% through eliminating unproductive products, strengthening cash flow. Product Mix Enhancement - Private label brands now represent approximately 20% of sales with superior margins compared to national brands. Forward Outlook: Management expects Q1 2026 revenue around $1.49 billion and full-year EBITDA guidance of $422.5 million, exceeding analyst estimates.
